hypothesis_en: Mr President, I would also like to thank, on behalf of the Members of the European Parliament from the European People's Party, for the countless expressions of solidarity and to thank you personally for your support and your company in times of pain. The plenary decided that it should be 11 March, and I congratulate myself that this proposal has been ratified by the Council. To meet these needs, my group has already requested the inclusion, for the first time in the 2004 budget, of a line of EUR 1 million to create a pilot programme to support victims of terrorism and their families. I would like to express here our intention to significantly increase the financial allocation of the pilot project to help victims, raising the scope of this project to a large-scale Community action. Mr President, ladies and gentlemen, on 11 September the Anti-Terrorism Action Plan was adopted, and yet on 11 March 2004 some of the measures envisaged there have not yet been implemented. I reiterate here the request made by several of my colleagues, because it is essential that the Member States cooperate in the swift incorporation of the measures adopted under the third pillar. I would like to conclude, Mr President, that from my country's experience, the defeat of terrorism is not easy, it requires persistence and tenacity, but it is an unavoidable challenge for all Europeans if we want to continue to live in free and democratic societies. We face it together, with democratic firmness and the strength of the rule of law.
reference_en: thank you President (1.89) on behalf of the Members of the European Parliament from the PP I would like to thank everybody for their expressions of solidarity and personally I would like to thank ~you_ for your support in our pain of course our solidarity lies with the victims and their families and that's why with other colleagues we took the initiative in ~the_ Committee of Citizens Freedoms of calling on the European Union to set a date to remember the victims of terrorism Plenary decided that this should be the eleventh of March and I welcome the fact that this proposal was in fact ratified by Council this was decided almost by unanimity Mr Ortuondo didn't support it he'll have his reasons and I'm sure he'll explain them but support for victims from European institutions mustn't just be moral it must also be material to cover those needs my group did in fact want the budget of two thousand and four for the first time to include a line of one million euros to set up a pilot programme for support for the victims of terrorism and for their family members circumstances unfortunately show that we need to do more and notwithstanding our support for the assistance for victims adopted by Council I would like to express here our intention to try and significantly increase the fine= allocation given to the pilot programme of aid to victims so that this project actually becomes a community action line that is taken seriously this is the commitment of the PP Group which was included amongst the political priorities for the next legislature which stipulates the setting up of a European fund for compensation for the victims of terrorism (1.66) ladies and gentlemen Members of the House since the eleventh of September we've adopted the action plan against terrorism and yet on the eleventh of March two thousand and four some of the measures outlined in that plan still hadn't been ~tran_sposed into national legislation let me reiterate here the appeal made by various colleagues because it's vital that the Member States cooperate in ensuring that such measures be transposed into national legislation under the third pillar it's vital that we improve security (1.05) in prevention in Europe can I recall in conclusion President that based on the experience we have in my country if we're to beat terrorism this isn't going to be easy it needs consistency and tenacity but it's absolutely vital that we do this if in Europe we're to continue to live in free and democratic societies we are all threatened here ~a_nd nobody can escape making difficult choices we will only be able to come through this challenge if we face it united democratically and backed up by the rule of law
source_es: sí gracias Presidente yo quiero también agradecer en nombre de los eurodiputados del Partido Popular Europeo las innumerables muestras de solidaridad y a usted personalmente agradecerle su apoyo y su compañía en los momentos de dolor por supuesto que nuestro permanente recuerdo está con las víctimas y con sus familiares y es por ello que con otros colegas tomamos la iniciativa en la Comisión de Libertades Públicas de recomendar a la Unión Europea que fijara un día en memoria y recuerdo de las víctimas el Plenario decidió que fuera el once de marzo y yo me congratulo que esa propuesta haya sido ratificada por el Consejo lo decidió casi por unanimidad el Señor Ortuondo no lo apoyó y él sabrá por qué y explicará por qué pero el apoyo a las víctimas desde las instituciones europeas no debe ser sólo moral sino también material para cubrir estas necesidades mi grupo ya solicitó la inclusión por primera vez en el presupuesto del dos mil cuatro de una línea de un millón de euros destinada a crear un programa piloto de apoyo a las víctimas del terrorismo y a sus familiares las circunstancias desgraciadamente demuestran que hay que hacer más y sin menoscabo de nuestro apoyo a las medidas de asistencia a las víctimas que el Consejo ha aprobado yo quiero expresar aquí nuestra intención de que se incremente significativamente la dotación financiera del proyecto piloto de ayuda a las víctimas elevando al rango de dicho proyecto a una acción comunitaria de gran envergadura ése es señor Presidente el compromiso del gru= del Grupo Popular que ha sido incluido en las prioridades políticas para la próxima legislatura y que prevé la creación de un fondo europeo de compensación para las víctimas del terrorismo señoras y señores eurodiputados tras el once de septiembre se aprobó el plan de acción contra el terrorismo y sin embargo el once de marzo de dos mil cuatro algunas de las medidas allí previstas todavía no han sido transcritas a los ordenamientos nacionales yo reitero aquí la petición que han hecho varios de los colegas porque es esencial la colaboración de los Estados miembros en la pronta incorporación de las medidas adoptadas en el marco del tercer pilar (1.12) es de vital importancia mejorar la seguridad en el sector de la prevención en Europa quisiera recordar para finalizar señor Presidente que desde la experiencia de mi país la derrota del terrorismo no es fácil exi= exige persistencia y tenacidad pero es un reto ineludible de todos los europeos si queremos seguir viviendo en sociedades libres y democráticas nadie está libre de esa amenaza y sería una tragedia que algunos quisieran escapar de ella mirando para otro lado al contrario la superaremos si la afrontamos juntos con firmeza democrática y la fuerza del estado de derecho
